signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + Inflation Shock
signals.text: RBNZ 2.5%. With NZD long% at a 15.2% extreme and Q2 CPI data due July 21, an upside surprise triggers a high-conviction short-squeeze. NZD/USD at 0.5841.
signals.watch level: New Zealand Q2 CPI release July 21

signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical Tension + Carry + Vol
signals.text: USD/JPY at 162.46 holding above the 160.00 intervention threshold as Trump-Iran escalation drives USD demand. 275bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid despite a -1.40 moderate put skew in 25d risk reversals. JPY safe-haven function remains impaired (z=-1.98σ vs VIX), meaning carry dominates until VIX exceeds 25.
signals.watch level: 160.00 psychological support and BoJ verbal intervention

signals.title: EUR/USD — Positioning + Vol + CESI
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1416 under pressure as CESI net -16 (persistent data misses) aligns with COT LF net short -74,637. IV at 7.1% vs HV30 at 4.7% creates a +2.4% VRP, signaling elevated hedging demand ahead of the July 23 ECB meeting. 150bp USD carry advantage limits upside potential.

signals.title: AUD/CHF — Retail Sentiment Extreme + Carry
signals.text: AUD/CHF retail sentiment at 93% short (extreme) diverges from the +435bp carry advantage favoring AUD. Retail is aggressively fading the RBA-SNB policy gap; contrarian logic suggests a continuation of the AUD bid as retail capitulation risk rises. AUD/USD at 0.7001.
